title: Greenhouse Effect Energy Balance
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Adjust CO2 and methane concentrations to see how greenhouse gases trap infrared radiation and raise Earth's surface temperature.

learningObjectives: Demonstrate how increasing greenhouse gas concentrations shift Earth's energy balance and raise surface temperatures
Explain the mechanism by which greenhouse gases trap outgoing infrared radiation
Compare the relative warming effects of CO2 and methane
Distinguish between the natural greenhouse effect and the enhanced greenhouse effect

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Slider-based experimentation with data collection and graphing
keyQuestions: How does the greenhouse effect keep Earth habitable, and what happens when it is enhanced?
Why does increasing CO2 from 280 to 560 ppm cause a different temperature change than 560 to 840 ppm?
How do CO2 and methane compare in their warming effect per molecule?
What would Earth's temperature be without any greenhouse gases?
commonMisconceptions: Students often confuse the greenhouse effect with the ozone hole, thinking they are the same problem
Many students believe the greenhouse effect is entirely harmful rather than a natural process enhanced by human activity
Students may think CO2 is the only greenhouse gas that matters
assessmentOpportunities: Evaluate student-created CO2 vs. temperature graphs for accuracy and interpretation
Assess written explanations of the greenhouse mechanism using correct scientific terminology
Compare student predictions about combined gas effects with simulation results
